tools.name: assess_trip
tools.description:
Decision support for ONE trip: "should I care?", answered honestly.

    Give the destinations and the travel window (date_from/date_to,
    YYYY-MM-DD). Destinations are the EU-27 ISO2 codes (Greece = "EL") PLUS the
    non-EU27 countries we actively monitor: Norway ("NO", rail via Entur, live),

tools.name: watch_trip
tools.description:
Watch a trip over time — the continuity primitive a chat cannot match.

    Persists the trip as a MONITORED OBJECT and returns its initial
    assessment plus a stable trip_id. Idempotent on identity: re-watching the
    same destinations+window refreshes the same trip rather than duplicating
    i

tools.name: whats_changed
tools.description:
What changed for a watched trip since last time — ONLY the delta.

    The continuity payload a memoryless chat cannot produce: recomputes the
    trip's current assessment and diffs it against the checkpoint at/just-
    before `since` (or the last evaluation when `since` is omitted). Returns
    {

tools.name: explain_silence
tools.description:
Why you heard nothing — the SILENCE AUDIT for a calm watched trip.

    Answers 'why didn't I hear from you?' honestly, which a stateless model
    cannot: {recheck_count, last_checked} (we WERE monitoring),
    {suppressed:[{what, domain, country, score, floor, reason}]} (what we saw
    for your d

tools.name: get_trip_updates_since
tools.description:
The watched-trip updates log filtered to entries AFTER `since`.

    The notification payload: everything logged for the trip strictly after
    the `since` ISO-8601 timestamp (the user's last-seen time), oldest-first.
    Returns {trip_id, since, new_update_count, updates}. Use this to push only

tools.name: country_briefing
tools.description:
Get today's published daily briefing, pre-formatted for end users.

    Returns the same payload as the underlying daily briefing artifact:
    headline counts, one plain-language headline per active event,
    monitoring coverage (sources_ok / blind_spots_count), backtest trust
    figures, and a c

tools.name: list_events
tools.description:
List detected travel-disruption events for EU-27 tourism.

    Events are deterministic, rule-based detections over published live
    snapshots and monthly indicators — thresholds are disclosed in each
    event record; nothing is model-generated here. Filters: status (e.g.
    "active"/"resolved")

tools.name: country_tourism_profile
tools.description:
Tourism profile for one EU-27 country — a decade of Eurostat data.

    Pure projection of the published artifacts (countries/<code>.json +
    competitiveness.json): yearly nights/arrivals totals with YoY, average
    stay, seasonality (peak month and peak-to-mean ratio, formula disclosed),
    dom

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "assess_trip" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Decision support for ONE trip: "should I care?", answered honestly. Give the destinations and the travel window (date_from/date_to, YYYY-MM-DD). Destinations ar…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "assess_trip" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Decision support for ONE trip: "should I care?", answered honestly. Give the destinations and the travel window (date_from/date_to, YYYY-MM-DD). Destinations ar…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "assess_trip" is 5106 chars, far longer than this server's median (785). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Decision support for ONE trip: "should I care?", answered honestly. Give the destinations and the travel window (date_from/date_to, YYYY-MM-DD). Destinations ar…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "watch_trip" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Watch a trip over time — the continuity primitive a chat cannot match. Persists the trip as a MONITORED OBJECT and returns its initial assessment plus a stable …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "v1" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: assess_trip, watch_trip, whats_changed, get_trip_updates_since · Private data: assess_trip, watch_trip, list_events · Public sink: watch_trip, country_tourism_profile · Destructive: watch_trip.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: assess_trip, watch_trip, whats_changed, get_trip_updates_since · Private data: assess_trip, watch_trip, list_events · Public sink: watch_trip, country_tourism_profile · Destructive: watch_trip

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
